Wrapped Ethereum (WETH) follows with a price of $1,806.14. Solana (SOL) is priced at $120.28, while the AI-focused token ai16z is trading at a significantly lower value of $0.1278.

categories.title: ElizaOS CLI Issues and Fixes: Test Command Bug and Windows Startup Errors

content.text: A recent pull request fixed a critical bug in the ElizaOS CLI where the `elizaos test` command would fail immediately after creating a new project or plugin using `elizaos create`. The issue, tracked under ELI2-188, caused `SyntaxError` and `TypeError` exceptions, preventing developers from running tests on newly scaffolded plugins like `plugin-twitter`. The fix ensures that test scaffolding and module exports are correctly set up, improving the developer onboarding experience. No documentation changes were required, and the fix was verified by running tests in a newly created plugin directory.

Users on Windows, particularly those using Node.js v23.3 via NVM, are encountering multiple issues when running `elizaos start`. The startup process fails due to missing or improperly resolved module paths, such as `fastembed` and `@elizaos/plugin-bootstrap`. Errors include invalid ESM loader paths (e.g., 'g:' protocol), missing `index.js` files, and failed plugin imports both locally and globally. Additionally, database migrations fail due to missing `meta/_journal.json` files. These issues significantly hinder the ability to launch ElizaOS on Windows environments and suggest compatibility or path resolution problems with the current CLI and plugin architecture.

categories.title: Development Updates and Local Development Guide Adjustment

content.text: A recent update focused on improving the local development guide. Specifically, the file local-development.md was relocated in commit aef7d45d01172227031e9ed3e53910b422cbc9bc, and this PR updates the relevant sources to reflect that change.

Development efforts also included package improvements, notably addressing a bun installation issue related to plugin-bootstrap. The update involved 5 contributors and merged 2 pull requests, with one bug fix and various other enhancements.

categories.title: Recent Fixes and Improvements in ElizaOS CLI and Plugin System

content.text: A bug affecting the `elizaos test` command was fixed. Previously, running tests immediately after creating a new plugin or project using `elizaos create` would result in `SyntaxError` and `TypeError` issues. This fix ensures that test scaffolding and module exports are correctly set up, improving the developer onboarding experience.

The `plugin-bootstrap` package was updated to remove a deprecated `postscript` entry that referenced a non-existent script. This change reduces the risk of post-installation errors and does not affect runtime behavior.

An issue was reported when running `elizaos start` on Windows with Node.js v23.3 installed via NVM. The startup process fails due to missing plugin modules and incorrect path handling in ESM imports. Errors include failed plugin imports, missing migration files, and unsupported URL protocols. This highlights compatibility issues with Windows environments and the need for improved plugin resolution and path handling.

A cleanup script was updated to improve cross-platform compatibility. The original use of `-print0 | xargs -0` was replaced with `-exec rm -rf {} +`, ensuring the script works on both Linux and macOS systems.
The local development documentation was updated to reflect a recent file relocation. This ensures that references to `local-development.md` remain accurate and up to date.

A performance improvement was made to the Twitter interaction logic. The system now reuses previously fetched mention data instead of making redundant API calls. Event emissions for mentions and reactions are currently disabled and will be addressed in a future update.
